fabricated_divergences.quote: Turkish-language coverage (Hurriyet) foregrounds Iranian military commanders' explicit threats of retaliation against the US and Israel and quotes multiple Iranian security officials, while English-language Western outlets (DW News, France 24, Japan Today) focus more on succession uncertainty, public dissent, and the regime's legitimacy crisis.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: The 'succession uncertainty' attribution to the Western trio is unsupported and inverted: it is the Turkish article (5) that reports uncertainty over whether Mojtaba will appear, while DW (2) covers legacy/dissent and France 24 (4) covers warnings, vengeance and the NYT report on targeting negotiators — not succession. France 24 does not foreground public dissent or a legitimacy crisis either.

fabricated_divergences.quote: Iranian state-linked outlets and their amplification via Turkish (Hurriyet) coverage emphasize official warnings against US/Israeli attack, calls for vengeance, and framing the funeral as a display of national unity and resilience, whereas UK-based Middle East Eye and Japan Today foreground internal dissent, private celebrations after Khamenei's death, and residents fleeing the mobilization.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: Middle East Eye articles (7, 9, 10) cover body arrival, Guterres/Iravani, and the Hormuz UNSC warning — none foreground internal dissent, private celebrations, or residents fleeing. Those themes appear only in Japan Today (article 6). Attributing the dissent framing to MEE is unsupported.

fabricated_divergences.quote: English-language Gulf and Western outlets (Al Jazeera, France 24, DW) foreground succession uncertainty around Mojtaba Khamenei and the funeral's leverage over nuclear diplomacy, while Turkish-language coverage (Hurriyet) focuses narrowly on military threats and retaliation rhetoric without addressing succession politics.
fabricated_divergences.why_unsupported: This is inverted: the Mojtaba succession uncertainty appears in the Turkish article (5), not in Al Jazeera (1, 8), DW (2), or France 24 (4). None of the named Gulf/Western analyses foreground Mojtaba succession or nuclear-diplomacy leverage, so the claimed contrast is fabricated and reversed.
